title: Reset Key (Shift+R) to Restart Game
content:
## Overview

Pressing **Shift+R** resets the snake game at any time — whether the game is running, paused, or on the game-over screen. The on-screen controls hint in `index.html` displays "Press Shift+R to Reset" so players can discover the shortcut without consulting external documentation.

## Implementation

**Files:** `src/main.ts`, `index.html`

### Key handler (`src/main.ts` — `handleKeyPress`)

A check for `e.key === 'R'` (uppercase, which the browser only produces when Shift is held) is placed at the **very top** of `handleKeyPress`, before the pause check and before any movement/direction logic. When matched, it calls `startNewGame()` immediately and returns early, so no other key logic runs. This guarantees the reset fires unconditionally from any game state.

### New-game routine (`src/main.ts` — `startNewGame`)

`startNewGame()` explicitly adds the `hidden` CSS class to the pause overlay element when a new game begins. This prevents a visual bug where resetting from a paused state would leave the "Paused" banner visible over the fresh game canvas. The fix lives in `startNewGame` rather than in the key handler so it applies regardless of how a new game is triggered.

### On-screen hint (`index.html`)

The controls hint text was updated to include "Press Shift+R to Reset".

## Behavior

| Game state at time of reset | Result |
|---|---|
| Running | Immediate reset; new game starts cleanly |
| Paused | Pause overlay is hidden; new game starts cleanly |
| Game over | New game starts cleanly |

## History note

An earlier iteration used plain (unmodified) **R** for reset. That was replaced by **Shift+R** after repeated revert/reapply cycles indicated that an unmodified single-key reset caused accidental resets during normal play (the snake is steered with letter keys, making a bare R easy to hit by mistake). The final merged implementation (PR #3) settled on Shift+R as the deliberate, low-accident-risk binding.
